canonical_question: What are the most common iOS app crash patterns and fixes?
constraints: iOS/iPadOS/watchOS/tvOS/visionOS only — Android crashes use different signals and tooling (logcat, tombstones)
Crash reports must be symbolicated before analysis — unsymbolicated reports show only hex addresses, not function names
dSYM files must match the exact build that crashed — mismatched dSYMs produce wrong or missing symbols
Watchdog terminations (0x8badf00d) do not produce crash reports in the normal sense — they are logged as termination events in Xcode Organizer
Never ship with Zombie Objects enabled — it prevents deallocation and causes unbounded memory growth in production

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Android app crash or ANR (Application Not Responding)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Android crash debugging — uses logcat, tombstones, and different signal handling

skip_this_unit_if.condition: C/C++ segmentation fault on Linux/macOS (not iOS)
React Native or Flutter crash on iOS
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Cross-platform framework crash debugging — JS/Dart exceptions need framework-specific tools before native analysis

skip_this_unit_if.condition: App rejected by App Review for crashes
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: App Store review crash resolution — focus on TestFlight Xcode Organizer crash triage

often_confused_with.label: iOS Performance Hangs (no crash — app freezes but does not terminate via exception)

inputs_needed.question: What exception type do you see in the crash report?
Is the crashing code Swift or Objective-C?
What iOS version is the crash occurring on?
